---
title: "Random Effect Assignment"
author: "Brian D. Gerber"
date: "2022-11-14"
output: html_document
---

<style type="text/css">
  body{
  font-size: 12pt;
}
pre{
  font-size: 20px; 
}
</style>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### **Objective**: To understand two types of *random effects*

### **Background**

We are interested in developing two types of linear trend models that could be useful to evaluating population increases/decreases of animal counts (e.g., Breeding Bird Survey).

In class, we will simulate count data 1) assuming a linear trend with extra-Poisson variation and 2) assuming site-level variation in the slope term.


### Model Fitting

You should fit the respective Bayesian model to each of these data. 

Plot the count data and your Bayesian predictions.
